title: Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium

records.question: What is Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium for?
records.answer_summary: It provides a baseline expectation for genotype frequencies under a simplified null model, so departures can guide questions about evolutionary forces or population structure.
records.verification_prompt: State the expected role of the model before interpreting any mismatch.
records.misconception_guard: Do not treat the equilibrium model as a claim that real populations never change.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Rewrite the explanation so the model is described as a baseline rather than a verdict.

records.question: Why are the assumptions important?
records.answer_summary: The model only works as intended when its assumptions are explicit; otherwise learners may attribute every deviation to the wrong cause.
records.verification_prompt: List the assumptions before naming an explanatory mechanism.
records.misconception_guard: Do not skip the assumptions and jump directly to one favorite cause.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Name the assumptions first, then say which one you think may be failing.

records.question: Does a Hardy-Weinberg departure automatically prove selection?
records.answer_summary: No. A deviation may reflect selection, but it can also reflect non-random mating, migration, drift, mutation, or sampling issues.
records.verification_prompt: Name at least two mechanisms that could explain the observed departure.
records.misconception_guard: Do not use deviation from equilibrium as automatic proof of selection.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Give one selection explanation and one non-selection explanation for the mismatch.

records.question: Why keep the allele-frequency perspective connected?
records.answer_summary: Hardy-Weinberg helps connect genotype patterns to allele-frequency reasoning, so learners can move between statistical expectation and evolutionary mechanism.
records.verification_prompt: Explain how genotype counts relate back to allele-frequency expectations.
records.misconception_guard: Do not treat genotype-frequency calculation as disconnected from evolutionary reasoning.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Rewrite the result so genotype observations and allele-frequency logic are linked.

records.question: Why is Hardy-Weinberg still useful if real populations are messy?
records.answer_summary: A good null model is useful precisely when reality departs from it in interpretable ways.
records.verification_prompt: State what question the departure lets you ask that you could not ask as clearly without the baseline.
records.misconception_guard: Do not dismiss the model just because the population is not idealized.
records.didactopus_prompt_seed: Describe one reason the null model helps even when the real case is more complicated.

citegeist_source_slots.needed_for: Original and foundational statements of Hardy-Weinberg expectations
Explanatory population-genetics treatments of Hardy-Weinberg logic and departures
candidate_queries: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium educational explanation population genetics
Hardy-Weinberg deviation selection drift mating migration explanation

doclift_use: Use this JSON as a fixture for concept pages that teach null-model reasoning and mechanism discrimination.
groundrecall_use: Store rationale, pending source-slot work, and revision notes about null-model interpretation so later Notebook pages can reuse the same evidence structure.
